title: Duplicate source class conversion functions with different semantics
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/app_interface/hal_bridge.c

Two functions convert HAL location source class to app source class but have different default behaviors:

1. app_location_source_class_from_hal() at lines 29-48:
   default: return LICHEN_APP_LOCATION_SOURCE_NONE;

2. app_source_class_from_hal() at lines 282-294:
   default: return LICHEN_APP_LOCATION_SOURCE_LOCAL_CLIENT;

The naming is nearly identical but semantics differ. This can cause confusion:
- Function 1 is used in lichen_app_location_time_from_hal() for reading from HAL
- Function 2 is used in submit_to_hal_as() for validation

If an unknown source class is encountered:
- Reading: maps to NONE
- Validation: expects LOCAL_CLIENT

This asymmetry may be intentional but should be documented. If not intentional, consolidate to a single function with consistent behavior.
notes: Consolidated duplicate HAL-to-APP location source class conversion into single function; default_source_name now uses it for consistent default semantics (NONE for unknown). Matches bead description.
